findings.title: Compatibility stays authoritative and presentation stays observational
findings.observation: Dust Bunny and Steps use different progression structures and art, but both require one unchanged-cart logical-update trace and identical HD-off versus HD-on compatibility state.
findings.resolution: Reuse Replay v1, the shared Wasm compatibility identity, complete logical-update execution, input projection, and state invariance without adding a game-specific simulation fork.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not encode a Steps checkpoint, coordinate, saved path, or Dust Bunny level rule in the compatibility kernel.

findings.title: Source visibility effects govern the finished semantic content route
findings.observation: Steps v19c reconstructed recognizable HD geometry but incorrectly displayed it through a source-authored darkness mask that hid the original map.
findings.resolution: Derive visibility applicability from source evidence, composite the finished semantic route under that mask, keep the framebuffer as visibility oracle rather than HD geometry, and reject a bypass mutation.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not assume a semantic redraw may ignore masking, palette, clipping, camera, or other source-authored visibility authority.

findings.title: Each Web game package owns a relative deployment base and cache scope
findings.observation: A Web-first game must remain playable when hosted below an arbitrary path, and sibling game packages must not overwrite one another's service-worker cache or navigation fallback.
findings.resolution: Resolve every package resource relative to the package base, derive service-worker navigation from registration scope, and include scope identity in the cache namespace.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not infer successful nested deployment or cache isolation from a root-hosted development server.

findings.title: Identity lineage and visible quality leap are separate mandatory gates
findings.observation: Across Dust Bunny and early Steps attempts, a correct coarse silhouette could still look like enlarged pixels, lose internal detail, blur a wordmark, or collapse distinct material layers.
findings.resolution: First bind source contour, topology, protected counters, material masks, and structural variants; then independently require continuous surfaces, shade/base/highlight detail, and density-aware supersampling.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not treat a matching bounding box, rounded rectangle, nearest-neighbour enlargement, or generic smoothing filter as an HD redraw.

findings.title: Source-drawn identity lettering and ordinary text take different routes
findings.observation: Dust Bunny and Steps both contain source-drawn lettering whose counters, spacing, and contour are part of visual identity, while ordinary labels require a readable fixed bundled font.
findings.resolution: Route wordmarks through contour-locked artwork and route semantic text through declared bundled fonts with deterministic metrics and no operating-system fallback.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not replace an identity-bearing wordmark with a convenient system font or render all source text as contour artwork.

findings.title: Steps gameplay marks require source-relative semantic classification
findings.observation: Dots, blocks, hazards, inventory marks, keys, doors, ocean layers, and status symbols cannot be classified reliably from isolated pixel shape or palette alone.
findings.resolution: Bind each reachable Steps role to cart logic, state transitions, scene context, and human semantic review inside the private adapter before authoring its HD recipe.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not turn the meaning of a Steps dot, square, or symbol into a universal mapping for unrelated carts.

findings.title: Steps keeps its own restrained maritime and mortality art direction
findings.observation: The accepted Dust Bunny reference uses warm rounded character language, while Steps depends on a restrained pale-on-navy composition, ordered frame layers, waves, vessel, terrain, and sparse symbolic cues.
findings.resolution: Preserve Steps source-relative hierarchy and atmosphere while modernizing contours, material finish, type clarity, and animation; do not import Dust Bunny's shapes or palette grammar.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not standardize every cart into rounded panels, cute faces, the Dust Bunny palette, or a shared decorative composition.

findings.title: Steps representative motion and gameplay cues remain a human judgment
findings.observation: Static same-state pairs cannot prove that story progression, torch visibility, inventory and door feedback, movement, danger cues, and the ending retain the original timing and meaning.
findings.resolution: Review the frozen v19e story, torch, inventory and door, and ending temporal sequences before deciding whether the supervised trial may advance to final scope.
findings.prohibitedGeneralization: Do not infer preserved play feel, motion, or cue readability from static identity checks or machine invariance alone.

limitations: This sanitized classification record is not a detached signed human decision, complete-game coverage, or publication authority.

The project owner approved the frozen v19e representative gameplay, motion, and cue packet and retained Steps as a bounded supervised trial through an interactive user message. Portable trusted-host authority and a rollback-resistant signed ledger head remain deployment work.
